name: Canvas (Freeform Workspace)
when_to_use: Use for spatial, open-ended creation where users arrange, connect, and manipulate objects on an unbounded 2D surface, such as design tools, diagramming, whiteboards, node editors, and flow builders.
when_not_to_use: Avoid for linear or list-based tasks, for read-mostly content, for small screens where panning a large canvas is awkward, or where a structured form or table would capture the data more reliably.
information_structure: An infinite or large bounded 2D plane holding freely positioned objects, with spatial relationships (proximity, connection, grouping) carrying meaning, surrounded by tool palettes and contextual inspectors.
navigation: Pan and zoom to move around; select and direct-manipulate objects; contextual toolbars and inspector panels edit the selection; mini-map or zoom-to-fit aids orientation.
responsive_behaviour: Poorly suited to small screens; offer a simplified view-only or limited-edit mode on mobile and reserve full canvas editing for larger pointer-driven displays.
accessibility_notes: Provide a keyboard-navigable alternative to select and move objects, since drag is not enough
Expose object names, roles, and relationships to assistive tech, not just visual position
Offer non-spatial entry points (lists, search) to reach objects without precise pointing
evidence: Figma, Miro, and node-based editors validate canvas for spatial creative work
Accessibility audits of canvas tools repeatedly highlight keyboard and screen-reader access as the central challenge
